commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] A focused automated repair can move the cap to the first body read and add targeted regression tests on the PR branch.; Needs attention: the intended OOM hardening is applied after the unbounded OAuth token body read, leaving the availability exposure in place.; Review finding: [P1] Move the cap before buffering the token body — extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.ts:219-224 (sha=c672490c2a829111b3536677692320f994ad9e0a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the OpenAI ChatGPT/Codex OAuth token exchange and refresh paths to use shared bounded response readers for token endpoint error text and success JSON parsing.

PR surface: Source +19. Total +19 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: Source-level yes, live replay no. A large token response reaches `postTokenForm` and is consumed by `response.arrayBuffer()` before any new bounded reader runs.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A focused automated repair can move the cap to the first body read and add targeted regression tests on the PR branch.

target.title: fix(openai): bound OAuth token response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## Summary
Replace unbounded response.text() in error paths and response.json() in success paths with readResponseWithLimit and readProviderJsonResponse (16 MiB cap) in OpenAI Codex OAuth token exchange and token refresh functions.

## Real behavior proof

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22, OpenClaw main @ 87db23e543

**Proof evidence**:
```
=== error path (readResponseWithLimit) ===
  chunks:17 cancelled:true PASS
=== success path (readProviderJsonResponse) ===
  chunks:17 cancelled:true PASS

PASS: both OpenAI OAuth paths bounded at 16 MiB
```

**All existing tests pass**: 10/10 in openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.test.ts.

## Risk
Low. The 16 MiB cap matches the codebase convention. Error paths use readResponseWithLimit + .catch(() => "") to preserve the existing graceful degradation pattern.
target.author: cxbasdev
